definition: A system that automates employee compensation processing including wage calculation, tax withholding, direct deposit, benefits deductions, and compliance reporting to tax authorities and regulatory agencies.
extendedDefinition: Payroll software eliminates the manual process of calculating wages, withholding taxes, and issuing payments while ensuring compliance with federal, state, and local payroll regulations. Modern payroll systems integrate with time tracking, HRIS, and accounting software to create an end-to-end compensation workflow. Key capabilities include automatic tax table updates (federal, state, FICA, Medicare, FUTA, SUI), year-end W-2 and 1099 processing, multi-state payroll for remote teams, garnishment handling, and benefits administration deductions (health insurance, 401k, HSA, FSA). Most payroll providers offer a tax penalty protection guarantee — if the software makes a tax filing error, the provider covers the resulting penalties.
examples: Gusto automatically calculating and depositing federal payroll taxes for a 15-person team across 5 states
ADP handling multi-state payroll compliance, direct deposit, and quarterly tax filings for a 200-employee company
A small business using QuickBooks Payroll to run weekly payroll, deduct health insurance premiums, and generate W-2s
description: Payroll software automates wage calculation, tax withholding, and direct deposit for employees. Learn how payroll systems reduce compliance risk and save time.
